scope: Profile-mode real Android Emulator user-origin 24 MiB attachment upload/download loopback through the server-wide gateway.

mobile_app.head: a507698
mobile_app.commit: test: add user upload stress avd hook

source.head: 7e436f7e
source.commit: fix: order mobile native transcript pages by record time

integration_smoke.stderr_warning: file_picker and mobile_scanner still use plugin-applied Kotlin Gradle Plugin; Flutter reports this as a future deprecation warning.

acceptance_notes: The run started two disposable real /home/bfly/yunwei/test_ccb2 projects and a loopback-only server-wide gateway.

The profile integration test opened real server-wide projects, exercised the existing small file/image/artifact matrix, then uploaded a 24 MiB user-origin text attachment on test_ccb2_beta/mobile_probe.
The app sent the attachment through the selected-agent composer, downloaded the resulting conversation attachment chip back into Android app documents, and verified matching SHA256.
The 24 MiB upload/download loop reported send-to-save latency of 6650 ms and post-chip download saved-visible latency of 1066 ms.
The raw log contains no FATAL, ANR, OutOfMemory, or skipped-frame markers.
residual_risks: This closes the local real AVD 24 MiB user-origin upload/download loopback hardening lane in profile integration mode.
Release-mode user-origin upload with the real Android system picker and physical-device/Tailnet transfer pressure remain separate P2 hardening lanes.
